name: Hasselle Copper Matte
notes: STAKEHOLDER NOTE (affects how to read $/lb): applied as a very thin coat -- just enough Ball Clay to make the Black Copper Oxide stick. The high copper density/availability at the surface is what produces the dramatic flame-reduction effects; that same density is what makes it costly at $11.43/lb. Per Jake, 2026-07-04: this $/lb figure overstates real per-piece cost since so little material is used per application -- do not compare this recipe to others on $/lb alone without accounting for coat thickness.
